info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A researcher is studying the life cycle of *Toxoplasma gondii* and notes that while many mammals can serve as hosts, domestic cats are uniquely classified as the definitive host. Which of the following processes occurring in the cat explains this classification?

options.label: Replication of the parasite within peripheral macrophages
Ingestion of undercooked meat from infected rodents
Occurrence of the sexual cycle and production of oocysts
Maturation of tachyzoites within skeletal muscle cells
Formation of tissue cysts containing bradyzoites

q2.prompt: While studying the pathogenesis of various intestinal nematodes, a medical student notes that some species require a heart-lung migration phase to complete their life cycle, often leading to eosinophilia. Which of the following organisms has complete lack of a tissue-invasive or pulmonary migration phase?

q3.prompt: A 35-year-old male with a history of HIV infection (CD4+ count 120 cells/mm³) presents with a 3-week history of worsening headache, low-grade fever, and focal neurological deficits. Physical examination reveals confusion and behavioral abnormalities. A lumbar puncture is performed, and the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis demonstrates mild pleocytosis with a predominance of lymphocytes, a high protein concentration, and a low glucose level. Which of the following findings is most likely to be observed on brain imaging for this patient?

options.label: Diffuse cerebral edema with no definite focal lesions

q4.prompt: During a microscopic stool examination of a patient with bloody diarrhea, a technologist identifies a trophozoite with a single nucleus showing a small central karyosome and fine, evenly distributed peripheral chromatin. The cytoplasm also contains several ingested red blood cells. Which organism has been identified?

q5.prompt: A patient with a protozoal infection is prescribed metronidazole. Which biochemical event must occur within the pathogen for this medication to produce its toxic, DNA-disrupting intermediates?

options.label: Inhibition of the dihydrofolate reductase enzyme

q6.prompt: A 24-year-old contact lens wearer presents with severe, progressive eye pain and blurred vision; physical examination reveals a corneal ulcer. Microscopy of a specific specimen shows wrinkled, thick-walled cysts. Which specimen is most appropriate for diagnosing this condition?

q7.prompt: A 40-year-old patient is concerned after noticing motile, ribbon-like segments in their stool. Laboratory examination of a recovered segment reveals a gravid proglottid with 22 lateral uterine branches. The patient most likely acquired this infection from consuming which of the following?

q8.prompt: A 42-year-old patient presents with a two-week history of watery diarrhea, fever, and significant weight loss. Stool microscopy reveals spherical, unsporulated oocysts measuring approximately 8-10 µm in diameter. These oocysts contain refractive greenish granules and exhibit bright blue autofluorescence when viewed under ultraviolet light microscopy. A modified acid-fast stain demonstrates variable staining against the blue background. Which of the following medications is the drug of choice for treating this infection?

q9.prompt: A patient with giardiasis presents with steatorrhea and evidence of vitamin B12 malabsorption. Which mechanism primarily explains how *Giardia lamblia* interferes with fat absorption in the small intestine?

options.label: Secretion of a specialized protease that degrades pancreatic lipase in the duodenal lumen
Physical obstruction of the biliary tree by the aggregation of trophozoites, preventing bile flow
Induction of mucosal apoptosis that selectively removes the microvilli responsible for lipid uptake
Use of a ventral sucking disc for attachment to the mucosa and the direct absorption of bile salts
Invasion of colonic enterocytes causing extensive cell death and ulceration

q10.prompt: A 46-year-old man receiving long-term corticosteroid therapy develops sudden onset of cough, dyspnea, and abdominal pain. Chest radiography shows new bilateral pulmonary infiltrates. An aspirate of endotracheal secretions is collected, and microscopic examination reveals numerous motile larvae with a notched tail. Blood cultures are positive for *Escherichia coli*. Which of the following describes the mechanism primarily responsible for the intensification of the parasite's life cycle and the secondary bacteremia in this patient?

options.label: Maturation of rhabditiform larvae into filariform larvae in the external environment followed by skin penetration
Internal transformation of rhabditiform larvae into infectious filariform larvae that penetrate the intestinal wall
Direct production of filariform larvae by adult worms within the pulmonary alveoli
Retrograde migration of mature adult worms from the jejunal mucosa into the pulmonary circulation
Hematogenous dissemination of adult female worms from the small intestine to distal organ systems
